⚛️ PQC Security Analysis Report

Post-Quantum Cryptography Migration Readiness Assessment

Target: /home/enishshah2/agentic-aama/.venv/lib/python3.11/site-packages/Crypto • Generated: 2026-06-29 01:54

📋 Executive Summary

1471
Critical Findings
1275
Moderate Findings
135
Low Risk
206
Files Scanned

This report analyzes the codebase for cryptographic algorithm usage and assesses the impact of large-scale quantum computing on security. Asymmetric cryptography (RSA, ECC, Diffie-Hellman) is completely broken by Shor's algorithm, while symmetric cryptography (AES) needs key size increases against Grover's algorithm.

🎯 Research Context

This tool was built as part of PhD research preparation in the Netherlands cybersecurity ecosystem. It addresses problems being actively researched at:

  • Radboud University (DiS): PQ-HINTS project — modeling PQC implementation vulnerabilities against side-channel attacks
  • TU Delft (Cybersecurity): Post-quantum cryptography migration strategies and network security
  • VU Amsterdam (VUSec): Formal verification of security protocols, including post-quantum protocols
  • UvA / QuSoft: Quantum network protocols and quantum-safe communication
  • TU Eindhoven: €21.5M CiCS program — PQC formal verification and threat intelligence

Reference: NIST IR 8413, ETSI TR 103 619, PQ-HINTS (Radboud)

🔍 Cryptography Scanner Results

Found 2881 instances of cryptographic algorithm usage across 206 files.

⏱️ Side-Channel Timing Analysis

Tests cryptographic operations for secret-dependent timing variations — the most common side-channel vulnerability.

OperationMean (ns)StdDev (ns)CVVerdict
❌ CRYSTALS-Kyber Encapsulation (constant-time ref) 394,096 425,338 1.0793 ❌ Leaks!
❌ CRYSTALS-Kyber Encapsulation (LEAKY implementation) 959,250 1,197,599 1.2485 ❌ Leaks!
